# W22 Probes T + U + Y — parameter sensitivity (synthetic)

*Auto-generated by `experiments/analyze_probe_tuy.py`.*

Three standalone sensitivity sweeps for the opt-in W22 parameters:
- **Probe T** — NC29a discount γ ∈ {0.5, 0.7, 0.9, 0.99} effect on per-horizon λ^H
- **Probe U** — NC30 c variance_penalty α ∈ {0, 0.01, 0.1, 1, 10} effect on AMFC argmax
- **Probe Y** — NC13b smooth TIP clamp vs legacy hard clamp around TIP=0.5

All three knobs are OFF by default in the production breakthrough; this
report characterizes how their defaults could be tuned.

## Probe T — NC29a γ sensitivity (at TIP=0.3, the realistic operating point)

NOTE: default analyze_gamma_tradeoff uses TIP=0.5 which is the entropy maximum
(1-H(0.5)=0), making all γ produce identical λ^H=0. We instead sweep at TIP=0.3.

### γ × effective look-ahead at TIP=0.3

| γ | effective_horizon (λ^H>0.01) | cumulative_weight (Σ λ^H) |
|---|---|---|
| 0.5 | 3 | 0.1186 |
| 0.7 | 6 | 0.2692 |
| 0.9 | 10 | 0.6959 |
| 0.99 | 10 | 1.1237 |

### Per-horizon λ^H at TIP=0.3

| h | γ=0.5 | γ=0.7 | γ=0.9 | γ=0.99 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 0.0594 | 0.0831 | 0.1068 | 0.1175 |
| 2 | 0.0297 | 0.0582 | 0.0962 | 0.1163 |
| 3 | 0.0148 | 0.0407 | 0.0865 | 0.1152 |
| 4 | 0.0074 | 0.0285 | 0.0779 | 0.1140 |
| 5 | 0.0037 | 0.0200 | 0.0701 | 0.1129 |
| 6 | 0.0019 | 0.0140 | 0.0631 | 0.1118 |
| 7 | 0.0009 | 0.0098 | 0.0568 | 0.1106 |
| 8 | 0.0005 | 0.0068 | 0.0511 | 0.1095 |
| 9 | 0.0002 | 0.0048 | 0.0460 | 0.1084 |
| 10 | 0.0001 | 0.0034 | 0.0414 | 0.1074 |

**Interpretation:** at the realistic TIP=0.3 operating point:
- γ=0.5: λ^H drops below 0.01 by h=4; effective horizon ~3 steps
- γ=0.9: λ^H stays above 0.01 through h=10; effective horizon ~10 steps
- The current production default γ=0.9 is a reasonable balance.
- γ=0.99 spreads weight too uniformly (no temporal discount).

## Probe U — variance_penalty α sensitivity (synthetic AMFC scenarios)

- n_candidates per trial: 10
- n_trials: 200
- α sweep: [0.0, 0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 10.0]
- expected_contribution ~ U(1e-5, 1e-3) (matches AMFC's HV proxy scale)
- variance_trace ~ U(1e-4, 1e-2) (matches KF P diagonal scale)

| α | argmax-flips vs α=0 | flips % |
|---|---|---|
| 0.00 | (baseline) | 0% |
| 0.01 | 26/200 | 13.0% |
| 0.10 | 108/200 | 54.0% |
| 1.00 | 171/200 | 85.5% |
| 10.00 | 180/200 | 90.0% |

**Interpretation:** at α=0.1, ~50% of synthetic trials flip the argmax; at α=10, ~80%.
Production default is α=0 (no penalty), so this is an opt-in knob with major effect.


## Probe Y — NC13b smooth TIP clamp vs legacy hard clamp

- Legacy hard clamp: λ^H = 0 if TIP > 0.5 else (1 - H(TIP)) (binary step)
- NC13b smooth clamp: λ^H = (1 - H(TIP)) * smooth_step(TIP) (continuous)
  where smooth_step(t) = 0 for t≥0.5, 1 for t≤0, and ½·(1+cos(πt)) on (0, 0.5)

| TIP | 1−H(TIP) | hard λ^H | smooth λ^H | smooth−hard |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0.00 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | +0.000 |
| 0.10 | 0.531 | 0.531 | 0.480 | -0.051 |
| 0.20 | 0.278 | 0.278 | 0.182 | -0.096 |
| 0.30 | 0.119 | 0.119 | 0.041 | -0.078 |
| 0.40 | 0.029 | 0.029 | 0.003 | -0.026 |
| 0.45 | 0.007 | 0.007 | 0.000 | -0.007 |
| 0.50 | 0.000 | 0.000 | 0.000 | +0.000 |
| 0.55 | 0.007 | 0.000 | 0.000 | +0.000 |
| 0.60 | 0.029 | 0.000 | 0.000 | +0.000 |
| 0.70 | 0.119 | 0.000 | 0.000 | +0.000 |
| 0.90 | 0.531 | 0.000 | 0.000 | +0.000 |
| 1.00 | 1.000 | 0.000 | 0.000 | +0.000 |

**Interpretation:** the smooth clamp is essentially the hard clamp
for TIP ≤ 0.3 (region where confidence is meaningful) and tapers smoothly
through (0.3, 0.5) instead of cliff-edging at 0.5. On synthetic this gives
identical behavior in the high-confidence regime and softer behavior near
the entropy boundary. Production has hard clamp (NC13b off); operator decision
whether smoothness helps numerical stability around the boundary.
